character_profile.name: Alley Oop MemEye Draft Curator
character_profile.persona_summary: A draft curator for visually grounded comic memory tasks built from page-level Alley Oop evidence.
character_profile.conversation_style: Short and factual. Emphasizes panel-level identity, location, and state changes.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track who appears, where they are, and how their state changes across panels.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 1 with five panels, including a crowned character in water and later panels showing the same crowned character again.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track repeated dinosaurs, collisions, and dialogue-linked actions across panels.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 2 with five panels showing Dinny, a crash into a tree, and a later discussion about clumsiness and trade-ins.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track the dinosaurs on display, signage, and the relative size of different dinosaurs.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 3 with dinosaur dealership panels, including a cheap dinosaur, a today's special dinosaur, and a smaller newer dinosaur.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track the rider, the smaller dinosaur, and visible state changes by the end of the page.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 4 with six panels showing Alley riding a smaller dinosaur, a test drive, and the dinosaur shown drooling in the final panel.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track the crowned character and his interactions with the shirtless man across the page.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 27 with a crowned character appearing across multiple panels and a final physical confrontation with a shirtless man.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track the crowned character, the temporary king, and the work he is forced to do later on the page.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 28 with the crowned character giving instructions, a temporary king being pelted by the crowd, and later doing work inside a cave.

dialogues.user: Memorize this comic page. Track the crowned character as he relaxes, gets punished, and later performs chores himself.
image_caption: Alley Oop page 29 with the crowned character relaxing with bon-bons, then being punished, then doing chores and running with a basin.

human-annotated QAs.question: In panel 1 and panel 5 of page 1, is the crowned character in the water both times? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
In panel 1 of page 1, is the character in the water wearing a crown? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 1, does the crowned character start in the water and end in the water by panel 5? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 2, does Dinny run into a tree before the conversation about his clumsiness? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
Across pages 2 and 3, is the dinosaur being considered for trade-in larger than the newer dinosaur shown at the dealership? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
At the dealership, is the dinosaur labeled as today's special smaller than the dinosaur marked cheap? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 27, does the crowned character appear in more than one panel? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 27, is the crowned character physically confronted by the shirtless man by the final panel? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 4, is Alley still riding the smaller dinosaur in both panel 1 and panel 5? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 4, by the final panel, is the dinosaur shown with drool coming from its mouth? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 28, after the crowd throws objects at the temporary king, is he later shown doing work inside a cave? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 28, is the crowned character the one giving instructions in panel 2 rather than the shirtless man? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
Across pages 28 and 29, is the crowned character first shown relaxing and later shown doing chores himself? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 29, are bon-bons shown next to the reclining crowned character in the first panel? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
On page 29, does the crowned character get struck by another person before he is shown running with a basin on his head later in the page? Reply with only yes or no in lowercase, with no punctuation.
